description_long: This fence is constructed from vertical wooden planks with a pronounced wood grain texture, arranged in a slightly irregular pattern that gives it a rustic, handmade appearance. The planks are fastened together by three horizontal black rails, which provide structural support and visual contrast. The top edge of the fence is uneven, with the tops of the planks cut at varying heights, creating an alternating, jagged silhouette. One plank on the right side features a metallic band, possibly indicating a repair or reinforcement. The overall build is lightweight and somewhat makeshift, suggesting it could be used as a temporary barrier or in informal urban settings such as construction sites, vacant lots, or community gardens. The gaps between the planks allow for partial visibility and airflow, while the rough finish and lack of ornamentation emphasize function over form.
description: A rustic wooden fence made of uneven vertical planks, joined by three black horizontal rails, with a jagged top edge and a visible metal band on one plank.
description_view_0: Front view showing the irregular vertical planks, three black horizontal rails, and a metal band on the rightmost plank.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the fence and the layered arrangement of the planks.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same plank arrangement and rails, but the metal band is less visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the staggered plank edges.
